<!-- GENERATED by mapping/_spec.py:format_markdown() — do not edit by hand. -->
# Shot Manifest — CSV Mapping File Format

A *mapping* is a small `.json` file that tells the Shot Manifest how to read a
particular CSV layout: which header columns mean what, which rows/values to
skip, how to resolve audio clips, and which default behaviors to attach.

## Adding your own

Built-in mappings ship read-only with the tool. **Your own** mappings live in
your writable templates folder — open it from the Shot Manifest header menu
with the *Open folder* button on the *CSV Mapping* row. The first time you open
an empty folder it's seeded with `example.json` (the skeleton at the bottom of
this page) and a copy of this reference, so you start with a model to copy and a
spec to read. Edit a renamed copy to match your CSV, then click *Refresh* to
pick it up — files are discovered automatically and appear in the mapping combo,
tagged by source. A user mapping whose name matches a built-in's **shadows** it,
so you can override a shipped mapping with one of your own.

## Top-level keys

Schema for one CSV-mapping file (see ``MAPPING_FORMAT.md``).

| Key | Required | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `columns` | no | Maps CSV header names to step fields. | see *ColumnMap* below |
| `audio_resolve` | no | How to find or derive an audio clip for each step (optional). | `{"method": "prefix", "directory": "//server/project/audio",…` |
| `default_behaviors` | no | Default behaviors per object kind, e.g. {"audio": ["set_clip"]} (optional). | `{"audio": ["set_clip"], "scene": ["fade_in"]}` |

### ColumnMap

Maps logical fields to CSV header names (case-insensitive).

| Key | Required | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `step_id` | no | Header alias(es) for the step-ID column. | `["Step"]` |
| `description` | no | Header alias(es) for the step description / contents column. | `["Step Contents", "Contents"]` |
| `assets` | no | Header alias(es) for the assets / object-names column. | `["Asset Names", "Asset"]` |
| `audio` | no | Header alias(es) for the audio / voice-over column (optional). | `["Voice Support", "Voice"]` |
| `exclude_steps` | no | Step IDs to skip entirely (e.g. setup rows). | `["SETUP"]` |
| `exclude_values` | no | Per-field cell values to treat as empty, e.g. {"assets": ["N/A"]}. | `{"assets": ["N/A"]}` |
| `metadata_pass` | no | Extra columns copied into shot metadata: {key: [header aliases]}. | `{"priority": ["Priority"]}` |

## `audio_resolve` methods

`audio_resolve.method` selects a strategy; each has its own required/optional keys:

| method | required | optional | description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `prefix` | — | directory, extensions | Match files named `{step_id}_*` in `directory`. |
| `regex` | pattern | directory, extensions | Match files against `pattern` (a `{step_id}` placeholder is substituted). |
| `map` | clips | — | Explicit `{step_id: clip_stem}` lookup table. |
| `derive` | — | words, separator, directory, extensions | Build the clip name from `step_id` + first N words of the audio text. |

`extensions` defaults to `[".wav", ".mp3"]`; `directory` is scanned for matches
(`map` needs no directory — it names clips directly).

## Skeleton (seeded as `example.json`)

```json
{
  "columns": {
    "step_id": [
      "Step"
    ],
    "description": [
      "Step Contents",
      "Contents"
    ],
    "assets": [
      "Asset Names",
      "Asset"
    ],
    "audio": [
      "Voice Support",
      "Voice"
    ],
    "exclude_steps": [
      "SETUP"
    ],
    "exclude_values": {
      "assets": [
        "N/A"
      ]
    },
    "metadata_pass": {
      "priority": [
        "Priority"
      ]
    }
  },
  "audio_resolve": {
    "method": "prefix",
    "directory": "//server/project/audio",
    "extensions": [
      ".wav",
      ".mp3"
    ]
  },
  "default_behaviors": {
    "audio": [
      "set_clip"
    ],
    "scene": [
      "fade_in"
    ]
  }
}
```
